name: Akita Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the warmth of the Akita lands and a deep red recalling local lacquerware
the snows of the Tohoku winter and purity

description: A dark red (maroon) field charged at the centre with a white emblem formed from a stylised katakana 'a' (ア), drawn with sweeping curves.
history: Akita's prefectural symbol was selected by public competition and adopted on 23 September 1959, with the flag (the symbol on a deep red field) in use from the same date.

features.symbolism: the deep red of the Akita region and its rice harvest

features.role: stylised katakana 'a' (ア) for 'Akita', drawn as a sweeping curve
features.symbolism: the katakana 'a' for Akita, rendered as a flowing form to evoke the prefecture's progress and the spread of its people.
